message:
chore: sync concurrent agent changes

Capture latest modifications from parallel agent development work.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

files.patch:
@@ -1457,7 +1457,7 @@
 {"id":"br-legjy.3.4","title":"C4: PTY regressions for event pressure + resize storms + input-latency safety","description":"## Intent
Lock in framework-level fixes with PTY regressions that simulate real terminal stress.

## Scope
1. Add traversal-under-pressure scenario (input + redraw activity).
2. Add resize-storm scenario with deterministic replay.
3. Measure input latency and render stability metrics.
4. Emit artifacts consumable by downstream project validation.

## Deliverable
Reusable PTY regression suite for frankentui event-loop health.","acceptance_criteria":"1. PTY suite covers event pressure and resize storms.
2. Input-latency and redraw metrics are captured per run.
3. Regressions are deterministic and actionable.
4. Artifacts integrate cleanly into consumer validation flows.
Plan-space hardening requirements:
1. Comprehensive unit tests must cover happy path, edge cases, boundary conditions, invariants, and failure paths introduced by this bead.
2. Comprehensive integration/E2E (PTY/stress where relevant) scripts must validate end-user behavior, degraded-mode behavior, and recovery paths for this bead.
3. Detailed diagnostics logging/artifacts are mandatory: scenario IDs, timestamps, timing breakdowns (p50/p95/p99 where applicable), failure reason codes, key counters, and artifact file paths.
4. Verification commands and reproduction steps must be documented so failures are locally reproducible by another engineer.","status":"open","priority":0,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-27T00:50:43.214250211Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-27T01:26:36.936199979Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["frankentui","incident","perf","testing","tui"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"br-legjy.3.4","depends_on_id":"br-legjy.3","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-27T00:50:43.214250211Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"br-legjy.3.4","depends_on_id":"br-legjy.3.3","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-27T00:53:57.344485921Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":950,"issue_id":"br-legjy.3.4","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"Ensure tests remain deterministic; flaky stress tests dilute incident confidence.","created_at":"2026-02-27T00:52:15Z"}]}
 {"id":"br-legjy.4","title":"Track D: mcp-agent-mail TUI hotpath corrections (scheduler, invalidation, overdraw)","description":"## Purpose
Remove app-layer hotpath mistakes in `mcp-agent-mail-server` that perform expensive work for inactive screens and over-render unchanged regions.

## Scope
1. Replace all-screen tick behavior with active-screen-centric scheduling plus explicit low-rate background maintenance.
2. Introduce dirty/invalidation semantics so unchanged screen state skips heavy recompute/render paths.
3. Stratify poller/data-refresh cadence by visibility and urgency.
4. Remove redundant clears/overdraw and unnecessary tiny write bursts where possible.
5. Ensure event queue/backpressure handling stays robust during bursts.

## Critical Constraint
No visual redesign; retain current UX output while drastically reducing wasted work.","acceptance_criteria":"1. Inactive screens no longer execute heavyweight tick/update loops at active cadence.
2. Dirty-state logic demonstrably reduces recompute and redraw volume.
3. Poller cadence respects data freshness needs without generating hot idle churn.
4. Rendering churn reduction does not alter screen appearance semantics.
Plan-space hardening requirements:
1. Comprehensive unit tests must cover happy path, edge cases, boundary conditions, invariants, and failure paths introduced by this bead.
2. Comprehensive integration/E2E (PTY/stress where relevant) scripts must validate end-user behavior, degraded-mode behavior, and recovery paths for this bead.
3. Detailed diagnostics logging/artifacts are mandatory: scenario IDs, timestamps, timing breakdowns (p50/p95/p99 where applicable), failure reason codes, key counters, and artifact file paths.
4. Verification commands and reproduction steps must be documented so failures are locally reproducible by another engineer.","status":"open","priority":0,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-27T00:48:28.238497299Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-27T01:26:42.551692306Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["hotpath","incident","perf","tui"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"br-legjy.4","depends_on_id":"br-legjy","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-27T00:48:28.238497299Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"br-legjy.4","depends_on_id":"br-legjy.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-27T00:53:52.098420543Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":936,"issue_id":"br-legjy.4","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"Known candidate blunder:  dispatch currently iterates all screen IDs. This track formalizes replacing that with visibility-aware scheduling and invalidation.","created_at":"2026-02-27T00:50:11Z"},{"id":990,"issue_id":"br-legjy.4","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"Known candidate blunder: Event::Tick currently iterates all screen IDs. Replace with visibility-aware scheduling plus explicit low-rate background maintenance.","created_at":"2026-02-27T00:54:55Z"}]}
 {"id":"br-legjy.4.1","title":"D1: Replace all-screen tick loop with visibility-aware scheduler in tui_app","description":"## Intent
Eliminate wasted per-tick work by removing all-screen active cadence in `tui_app`.

## Scope
1. Replace global all-screen tick dispatch with active-screen-first scheduling.
2. Introduce low-rate background maintenance for inactive screens only where needed.
3. Preserve behavior for time-sensitive features (toasts, overlays, alerts).
4. Add instrumentation proving reduced inactive work.

## Deliverable
Visibility-aware scheduler in app layer with behavior parity.","acceptance_criteria":"1. Inactive screens are no longer ticked at active cadence by default.
2. Active-screen responsiveness is equal or better than baseline.
3. Overlay/system features still update correctly.
4. Instrumentation shows reduced tick workload and CPU.
Plan-space hardening requirements:
1. Comprehensive unit tests must cover happy path, edge cases, boundary conditions, invariants, and failure paths introduced by this bead.
2. Comprehensive integration/E2E (PTY/stress where relevant) scripts must validate end-user behavior, degraded-mode behavior, and recovery paths for this bead.
3. Detailed diagnostics logging/artifacts are mandatory: scenario IDs, timestamps, timing breakdowns (p50/p95/p99 where applicable), failure reason codes, key counters, and artifact file paths.
4. Verification commands and reproduction steps must be documented so failures are locally reproducible by another engineer.","status":"closed","priority":0,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-27T00:50:43.526622834Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-27T05:05:59.154952229Z","closed_at":"2026-02-27T05:05:59.154932873Z","close_reason":"done","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["hotpath","incident","perf","tui"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"br-legjy.4.1","depends_on_id":"br-legjy.1.2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-27T01:25:28.320474867Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"br-legjy.4.1","depends_on_id":"br-legjy.4","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-27T00:50:43.526622834Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":951,"issue_id":"br-legjy.4.1","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"Candidate blunder already identified: Event::Tick path loops over ALL_SCREEN_IDS; this bead removes that structural waste.","created_at":"2026-02-27T00:52:15Z"},{"id":1002,"issue_id":"br-legjy.4.1","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"RedValley: Visibility-aware tick scheduler implemented in tui_app.rs. Active screen always ticked; inactive every 12th frame (INACTIVE_SCREEN_TICK_DIVISOR=12). Tests updated. cargo check passes.","created_at":"2026-02-27T05:05:55Z"}]}
-{"id":"br-legjy.4.2","title":"D2: Add dirty-state/invalidation contract for all screen models","description":"## Intent
Introduce strict dirty/invalidation semantics so unchanged state does not trigger expensive recompute.

## Scope
1. Define a shared invalidation contract for screen models.
2. Mark state transitions with precise dirty reasons.
3. Skip heavy recompute/render paths when no relevant invalidation occurred.
4. Add tests for false-positive/false-negative invalidation behavior.

## Deliverable
Deterministic invalidation model that cuts redundant work without stale UI bugs.","acceptance_criteria":"1. Screens can skip recompute/render on no-op ticks safely.
2. Dirty reasons are explicit and testable.
3. No stale-state or missed-update regressions occur.
4. Redundant update frequency is measurably reduced.
Plan-space hardening requirements:
1. Comprehensive unit tests must cover happy path, edge cases, boundary conditions, invariants, and failure paths introduced by this bead.
2. Comprehensive integration/E2E (PTY/stress where relevant) scripts must validate end-user behavior, degraded-mode behavior, and recovery paths for this bead.
3. Detailed diagnostics logging/artifacts are mandatory: scenario IDs, timestamps, timing breakdowns (p50/p95/p99 where applicable), failure reason codes, key counters, and artifact file paths.
4. Verification commands and reproduction steps must be documented so failures are locally reproducible by another engineer.","status":"in_progress","priority":0,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-27T00:50:43.843081054Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-27T05:07:23.652969641Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["incident","invalidation","perf","tui"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"br-legjy.4.2","depends_on_id":"br-legjy.4","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-27T00:50:43.843081054Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"br-legjy.4.2","depends_on_id":"br-legjy.4.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-27T00:53:57.979645914Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":952,"issue_id":"br-legjy.4.2","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"Invalidation bugs can be subtle; require tests that prove both no overwork and no stale display.","created_at":"2026-02-27T00:52:16Z"}]}

 {"id":"br-legjy.4.3","title":"D3: Stratify poller cadence (active/high-priority vs inactive/low-priority)","description":"## Intent
Reduce polling churn by applying cadence classes based on visibility and urgency.

## Scope
1. Define cadence tiers (active, nearby, inactive, background).
2. Map pollers/data refresh jobs to tiers with freshness justifications.
3. Ensure urgent data paths can bypass slower cadence when required.
4. Validate cadence policy under rapid tab traversal.

## Deliverable
Poller strategy that cuts idle CPU while preserving operational freshness.","acceptance_criteria":"1. Poller cadence tiers are implemented and documented.
2. Inactive/background polling load drops materially.
3. Urgent updates still surface within acceptable latency.
4. Traversal latency improves without data-staleness regressions.
Plan-space hardening requirements:
1. Comprehensive unit tests must cover happy path, edge cases, boundary conditions, invariants, and failure paths introduced by this bead.
2. Comprehensive integration/E2E (PTY/stress where relevant) scripts must validate end-user behavior, degraded-mode behavior, and recovery paths for this bead.
3. Detailed diagnostics logging/artifacts are mandatory: scenario IDs, timestamps, timing breakdowns (p50/p95/p99 where applicable), failure reason codes, key counters, and artifact file paths.
4. Verification commands and reproduction steps must be documented so failures are locally reproducible by another engineer.","status":"in_progress","priority":0,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"IcyAnchor","created_at":"2026-02-27T00:50:44.152701319Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-27T05:16:50.440030988Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["incident","perf","poller","tui"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"br-legjy.4.3","depends_on_id":"br-legjy.4","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-27T00:50:44.152701319Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"br-legjy.4.3","depends_on_id":"br-legjy.4.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-27T00:53:58.301640720Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":953,"issue_id":"br-legjy.4.3","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"Do not globally slow pollers; use classed policy with explicit urgency exceptions.","created_at":"2026-02-27T00:52:17Z"}]}
 {"id":"br-legjy.4.4","title":"D4: Remove redundant clears/overdraw and reduce write-amplification without visual drift","description":"## Intent
Reduce render write amplification and overdraw while preserving appearance.

## Scope
1. Identify redundant area clears/full repaints and eliminate unnecessary ones.
2. Minimize tiny repetitive ANSI writes where semantically redundant.
3. Keep style/output fidelity identical from user perspective.
4. Add visual-diff safeguards to prevent accidental UI drift.

## Deliverable
Lower redraw cost with no perceived visual change.","acceptance_criteria":"1. Redundant clear/repaint frequency drops in instrumentation.
2. Output byte volume per traversal decreases materially.
3. Visual parity checks pass (no intentional style/layout changes).
4. Flashing attributable to repaint churn is reduced.
Plan-space hardening requirements:
1. Comprehensive unit tests must cover happy path, edge cases, boundary conditions, invariants, and failure paths introduced by this bead.
2. Comprehensive integration/E2E (PTY/stress where relevant) scripts must validate end-user behavior, degraded-mode behavior, and recovery paths for this bead.
3. Detailed diagnostics logging/artifacts are mandatory: scenario IDs, timestamps, timing breakdowns (p50/p95/p99 where applicable), failure reason codes, key counters, and artifact file paths.
4. Verification commands and reproduction steps must be documented so failures are locally reproducible by another engineer.","status":"open","priority":0,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-27T00:50:44.466703846Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-27T01:26:35.361176251Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["incident","perf","render","tui"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"br-legjy.4.4","depends_on_id":"br-legjy.4","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-27T00:50:44.466703846Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"br-legjy.4.4","depends_on_id":"br-legjy.4.2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-27T00:53:58.619467645Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":954,"issue_id":"br-legjy.4.4","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"Optimization target is overdraw and write amplification, not theme/layout restyling.","created_at":"2026-02-27T00:52:17Z"}]}
 {"id":"br-legjy.4.5","title":"D5: Harden event-queue backpressure and burst handling during rapid tab traversal","description":"## Intent
Ensure bursty tab navigation cannot overload event processing and create lag cascades.

## Scope
1. Audit event queue growth/backpressure under rapid screen switches.
2. Add guards against pathological event accumulation and starvation.
3. Preserve ordering/semantic correctness of critical events.
4. Validate under stress scenarios with deterministic replay.

## Deliverable
Backpressure-safe event handling with stable traversal latency.","acceptance_criteria":"1. Event queue remains bounded under rapid traversal stress.
2. No starvation of key input or critical system events occurs.
3. Traversal latency remains within budget during burst input.
4. Stress artifacts demonstrate stable behavior.
Plan-space hardening requirements:
1. Comprehensive unit tests must cover happy path, edge cases, boundary conditions, invariants, and failure paths introduced by this bead.
2. Comprehensive integration/E2E (PTY/stress where relevant) scripts must validate end-user behavior, degraded-mode behavior, and recovery paths for this bead.
3. Detailed diagnostics logging/artifacts are mandatory: scenario IDs, timestamps, timing breakdowns (p50/p95/p99 where applicable), failure reason codes, key counters, and artifact file paths.
4. Verification commands and reproduction steps must be documented so failures are locally reproducible by another engineer.","status":"open","priority":0,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-27T00:50:44.788075273Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-27T01:26:34.959992558Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["events","incident","perf","tui"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"br-legjy.4.5","depends_on_id":"br-legjy.3.3","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-27T00:53:59.263957643Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"br-legjy.4.5","depends_on_id":"br-legjy.4","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-27T00:50:44.788075273Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"br-legjy.4.5","depends_on_id":"br-legjy.4.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-27T00:53:58.941322738Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":955,"issue_id":"br-legjy.4.5","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"This closes the loop between scheduler fairness and user-perceived lag during aggressive tabbing.","created_at":"2026-02-27T00:52:18Z"}]}

@@ -155,7 +155,10 @@ pub async fn macro_start_session(
         agent.name.clone(),
         Some(false),
         None,
-        Some(inbox_limit.unwrap_or(10)),
+        Some(match inbox_limit {
+            Some(l) if l > 0 => l,
+            _ => 10,
+        }),
         Some(false),
         None,
     )
@@ -331,7 +334,10 @@ pub async fn macro_prepare_thread(
         agent.name.clone(),
         Some(false),
         None,
-        Some(inbox_limit.unwrap_or(10)),
+        Some(match inbox_limit {
+            Some(l) if l > 0 => l,
+            _ => 10,
+        }),
         Some(include_inbox_bodies.unwrap_or(false)),
         None,
     )
